root_cause.text: The plugin improperly sanitized HTTP header values by replacing whitespace and newline characters with empty strings instead of spaces, failing to adequately neutralize CRLF sequences and allowing arbitrary HTTP header injection.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `FileTransfer.java` changes the sanitization of `headerValue` from `replaceAll("\\s+", "").replaceAll("\\n","")` to `replaceAll("\\s+", " ").replaceAll("\\n"," ")`, ensuring that whitespace and newline characters are safely replaced with spaces.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The plugin improperly sanitized HTTP header values by replacing whitespace and newline characters with empty strings instead of spaces, failing to adequately neutralize CRLF sequences and allowing arbitrary HTTP header injection.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `FileTransfer.java` changes the sanitization of `headerValue` from `replaceAll("\\s+", "").replaceAll("\\n","")` to `replaceAll("\\s+", " ").replaceAll("\\n"," ")`, ensuring that whitespace and newline characters are safely replaced with spaces.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix header value formatting by replacing whitespace and newlines with spaces instead of empty strings.
intent_record.what: Modified regex replacements to substitute whitespace and newlines with spaces instead of empty strings, and removed an extra semicolon.
intent_record.how: Prevents words in HTTP headers from being improperly concatenated by ensuring that whitespace and newlines are replaced with spaces.

grouping_result.intent: Fix header value formatting by replacing whitespace and newlines with spaces instead of empty strings.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly fixes the root cause of the CRLF injection vulnerability by replacing whitespace and newlines in HTTP headers with spaces instead of empty strings.

candidates.rationale: This line improperly replaces whitespace and newline characters with empty strings, failing to adequately neutralize CRLF sequences and allowing arbitrary HTTP header injection.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement improperly replaces whitespace and newline characters with empty strings instead of spaces, failing to neutralize CRLF sequences and allowing arbitrary HTTP header injection.

bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the vulnerable logic by replacing whitespace ('\\s+') and newline ('\\n') characters with empty strings ('') instead of spaces in 'finalValue = headerValue.replaceAll("\\s+", "").replaceAll("\\n","").replaceAll("[^\\x20-\\x7E]+", " ");', which matches the vulnerability root cause.

bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces a cookie manager abstraction and modifies how cookies are retrieved and set on the HTTP connection. The specific vulnerable logic mentioned in the root cause (improperly sanitizing HTTP headers by replacing whitespace and newline characters with empty strings) is not present in this code.
